source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/참고자료/참고 - 2,3G FDA 문서/VOL_009_Appendix H Biocompatibility Test Report/002_Biocompatibility Assessment Report.pdf

document.title: Biocompatibility Assessment Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer biocompatibility assessment for A1417MCW/A1717MCW/F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors, using FDA biocompatibility guideline and ISO 10993 test selection.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports biological safety assessment and test selection for limited skin-contact medical device use.
keyPoints: Device contact categorization is skin surface with limited exposure of 24 hours or less.
Selected biological evaluation tests are cytotoxicity, sensitization, and irritation or intracutaneous reactivity.
Korea Testing Laboratory reports 20-0094, 20-0095, and 20-0096 are listed as satisfied.
Final assessment states the devices are suitable to use as medical device and biological safety based on the results.

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: Selection of biological evaluation test according to FDA biocompatibility guideline

concepts.name: Biocompatibility Assessment
concepts.evidence: The purpose of this assessment is to ensure that no undue risks arise from biological hazards associated with materials of manufacture and final devices.

concepts.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
concepts.evidence: a) Common name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

concepts.name: Skin surface contact
concepts.evidence: a) Nature of body contact: Skin surface

concepts.name: Limited exposure
concepts.evidence: b) Duration of contact: Limited exposure (≤ 24 Hours)

evidence.claim: The document is a Biocompatibility Assessment Report with file number A-TCF-5-144 and revision 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Biocompatibility Assessment Report File No A-TCF-5-144 Rev. No 0

evidence.claim: The assessed devices are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The devices are indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and are not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems. Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital flat panel X-ray detector indications for use

evidence.claim: The contact part is the Deco sheet.
The device body-contact category is skin surface.
evidence.evidenceText: a) Nature of body contact: Skin surface

evidence.claim: The device contact duration is limited exposure of 24 hours or less.
evidence.evidenceText: b) Duration of contact: Limited exposure (≤ 24 Hours)

evidence.claim: The report selects cytotoxicity, sensitization, and irritation or intracutaneous reactivity as biological evaluation tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Cytotoxicity O Sensitization O Irritation or Intracutaneous Reactivity O

evidence.claim: Applied standards include ISO 10993-1, ISO 10993-5, ISO 10993-10, and ISO 10993-12.
evidence.evidenceText: 5. Applied Standards - ISO 10993-1: Selection of Tests - ISO 10993-5: Test for Cytotoxicity - ISO 10993-10: Test for Irritation and Sensitization - ISO 10993-12: Sample Preparation

evidence.claim: KTL test reports for cytotoxicity, irritation or intracutaneous reactivity, and sensitization are listed as satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Cytotoxicity ISO 10993-5 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0094 Satisfied; Irritation or Intracutaneous Reactivity ISO 10993-10 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0095 Satisfied; Sensitization ISO 10993-10 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0096 Satisfied

evidence.claim: The final assessment concludes that A1417MCW/A1717MCW/F1417MCW is suitable to use as medical device and biological safety based on the results.
evidence.evidenceText: In conclusion, A1417MCW/A1717MCW/F1417MCW is suitable to use as medical device and biological safety based on the above results.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: source metadata signals FDA and the document references an FDA biocompatibility guideline, but the report itself appears to be a manufacturer assessment by H&abyz Co., Ltd., not an FDA-issued document.
No FDA clearance decision, submission status, or 510(k) determination is stated in the provided text.
Some table text shows possible OCR or extraction artifacts, such as 'No .' and 'tpolycarbonate', so chemical composition details should be reviewed against the source PDF if used.
